text.title: Quality Attribute Tactics
text.summary: Proven design decisions (tactics) for performance, availability, security, modifiability, and scalability.

list.item: Increase resources: vertical scaling (more CPU/memory) — immediate but has a ceiling
Introduce concurrency: async I/O, thread pools, parallel processing — scales with workload
Reduce overhead: connection pooling, request batching, protocol efficiency
Bound queue sizes: backpressure — prevents resource exhaustion under overload
Schedule resources: priority queues — ensures critical requests are served first
Cache: CDN, Redis, local in-process — reduces load on downstream services
Ping/Echo: health checking by load balancer — removes failed instances automatically
Active redundancy: hot standby multi-AZ deployment — failover in seconds
Exception handling: circuit breakers, graceful degradation — prevents cascade failures
Rollback: database PITR, blue-green deployment — restores previous state after bad deploy
Voting: Raft/Paxos consensus — prevents split-brain in distributed systems
Authenticate: OAuth 2.0, OIDC — verify identity at every entry point
Authorize: RBAC, ABAC — verify permissions per resource
Encrypt: TLS for transit, AES-256 for rest — protect data in all states
Limit access: firewalls, VPC, zero-trust network — reduce attack surface
Detect intrusion: SIEM, WAF, anomaly detection — alert on active threats
Verify integrity: HMAC, digital signatures — detect tampering
Split module: microservices, separate packages — isolate change blast radius
Encapsulate: interfaces, APIs — hide implementation details from consumers
Use intermediary: message queues, event bus — decouple producers from consumers
Defer binding: feature flags, runtime configuration — enable changes without redeploy
Abstract services: adapters, ports-and-adapters — swap implementations without changing callers
Horizontal scaling: auto-scaling groups — add instances to match load
Data partitioning: hash/range sharding — distribute data across nodes
Stateless design: JWT tokens, no sticky sessions — enable any instance to handle any request
Async processing: message queues — decouple request rate from processing rate
Read replicas: read-heavy workloads — distribute read load away from primary
